name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA)
summary: Canada's federal private-sector privacy law governing how commercial organizations collect, use, and disclose personal information in the course of for-profit activities. PIPEDA applies across all provinces for federally regulated industries and in provinces without substantially similar legislation.
keyRequirements: Obtain meaningful consent for the collection, use, or disclosure of personal information
Limit collection to purposes a reasonable person would consider appropriate
Provide individuals with access to their personal information upon request
Allow individuals to challenge the accuracy and completeness of their data
Protect personal information with safeguards appropriate to the sensitivity
Appoint a privacy officer accountable for the organization's compliance
Mandatory breach reporting to the Office of the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals
penalties: Up to $100,000 CAD per violation for offenses such as obstructing investigations, retaliating against whistleblowers, or failing to report breaches. Enforced by the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada.
